channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Best Nanally Build Guide in Neverness to Everness—Arcs, Cartridges & More
item.content:encoded:
Neverness to Everness has a vast array of characters to recruit, but Nanally stands among the very best, and the best build can enhance her strong damage capabilities.

Nanally is an S-Rank character in Neverness to Everness, and is among the options you can select from the free S-Rank pick after pulling 50 times in the gacha system, and is a solid choice for any player.

If you’ve got Nanally at your disposal in Neverness to Everness and want to ensure you get the best out of the character, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

Best Neverness to Everness Nanally Build

The best build for Nanally in Neverness to Everness is based around increasing her attacking output, particularly targeting enemies in the Broken state.

The Song of the Whale Arc is a top choice, increasing attacking output in general, with further damage boosts to enemies with the Broken status, along with the benefit of restoring 30% of maximum HP if an enemy with the Broken status is defeated.

  • More Insider Gaming: Can You Romance Characters in Neverness to Everness?

As for Cartridge, Firefles and the Forest is the best option, with the Epic bonus boosting Anima damage, while the Legendary boost increases Crit DMG when a nearby enemy takes Anima damage, stacking multiple times, with the effects remaining even if Nanally is off-field herself.

Check out the table below for a full breakdown of the best Nanally build, including the effects provided.

Nanally Awakening Skills Unlock Order in Neverness to Everness

The first priority for Awakening Skills with Nanally is to unlock Call Me the Boss, followed by Because We’re Family, Follower’s Everywhere, and Gang Formation.

Call Me the Boss increases damage output when she is the Ichi-Daime Authority, a state she often enters, while Because We’re Family increases the uptime of the state.

Followers Everywhere provides further damage boosts whenever she performs a follow-up attack, while Gang Formation allows for quicker charging of her Ultimate Skill.

The table below shows the best order to unlock Awakening Skills in Neverness to Everness.

Best Nanally Team Composition in Neverness to Everness

The best team composition for Nanally in Neverness to Everness is built around using her as a main DPS, with Esper Zero providing alternative DPS, and Sakiri and Adler rounding out the team.

Both Esper Zero and Adler are available through in-game progression, so you won’t need to rely on RNG with pulls to add them to your roster, while you can get either Nanally or Sakiri through the free S-Tier pick once you have pulled 50 times.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Free Characters in Neverness to Everness & How to Get Them

Sakiri’s strong buffs make her the ideal support, with Adler providing shields and smaller inputs of damage, which can help set up Nanally’s offensive onslaught, making even the most challenging of battles a breeze.

If you’re yet to unlock Sakiri, Haniel is a decent alternative, with the added benefit of also being available to unlock for free.

What’s your favourite feature in Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Neverness to Everness, find out how to play with friends in multiplayer, and check out all of the active codes to redeem.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Fallout 76 Developers Have ‘Ideas For Years Down The Road’
item.content:encoded:
If you thought Fallout 76 was running low on evolution, think again. During the BAFTA Games Awards in London, we caught up with Bethesda Game Studios’ Jon Rush, Creative Director on Fallout 76, to learn about the expansive future in store for the game.

In his words, there’s so much in the tank, it’s becoming a problem. The team is bursting with ideas to push Fallout 76 further, and with the game’s tenth anniversary approaching in 2028, big things are on the horizon.

What’s Next For Fallout 76?

In December 2025, Bethesda rolled out the Burning Springs update in Fallout 76, the most substantial update to ever hit the platform. It linked the game with the television show, introduced a new map area in Ohio, and kick-started a new stretch of missions to expand the base offering for free (as always).

This year, the Backwoods update introduced Bigfoot, of all things.

Things continue to evolve, and there’s plenty in store for Fallout 76. In fact, there’s more lined up than players could ever know. In the words of Jon Rush, Creative Director, the problem is having too much to jam into the content pipeline:

When we’re so full of creative and talented people as we are at Bethesda Game Studios, it’s more of a problem of having too many great ideas and only so much time over the course of the year to do them.

We have ideas for years down the road. We’re making the game bigger, better, more fun, more accessible, so it only gets better from here.

If there’s one thing Bethesda Game Studios has done so well with Fallout 76, it’s that evolutionary piece. The game has expanded immeasurably over the years, and it’s a night-and-day comparison to where things were back in 2018.

What launched as a barren title now boasts more content than you could know, if you’re entirely unfamiliar with the game.

You’ve got a lengthy campaign, world events, hundreds of side and daily quests, raids, out-of-map expeditions, base map expansions, bounty hunts, fishing minigames, base building, and much more. There’s plenty to get stuck into, and if Jon Rush is to be believed (he’s a nice guy, take it from me), then, in the words of D:Ream, things can only get better.

Are you a Fallout 76 fan? Let us know what you’re excited about in the game’s future over on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that an Xbox VP has clarified ‘first-party’ Helix rumors

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: How Long Is Invincible VS Story Mode? Game Length & Difficulties, Explained
item.content:encoded:
As a fighting game, Invincible VS has multiple game modes, with the likely expectation being for you to invest countless hours in the Arcade and online multiplayer. However, for a pure cinematic experience with a single-player focus, the Invincible VS Story Mode is there.

The pantheon of fighting greats has a new contender: Invincible VS.

Based on the long-running comic, the beat ’em up features an extensive character roster with multiple modes. One of which you’re bound to play is the Story Mode, so get the lowdown on it before you play.

Invincible VS Story Mode Game Length, Explained

It took one of Insider Gaming’s writers just one hour to complete the Invincible VS Story Mode on Hard.

If you’re thinking of picking up Invincible VS solely for the Story Mode, then at $49.99, this is highly NOT recommended. Most people won’t anyway, as fighting game Story Modes are always just extra content ot enrich the overall experience.

  • More Insider Gaming: 7 New Invincible VS DLC Characters Have Leaked

But yes, we can confirm that an average Story Mode playthrough will clock in at around one hour.

The difficulty you choose and how well-versed you are in fighting games might have a bearing on your runtime, but even if you opt for the hardest difficulty, you shouldn’t be looking at more than two hours, tops.

Story Mode is fun and brings the series’ characters to life with breathtaking cutscenes and flash visuals, but there’s only so much Pow! to go around.

How Many Difficulty Levels in Invincible VS Story Mode?

The Invincible VS Story Mode has four different difficulty levels to choose from: Easy, Normal, Hard, and Viltrumite.

If you’ve played fighting games before, it’s the usual case here: Easier difficulties are more forgiving, and the AI responses are slower and less complex, whereas the latter end of the scale will see the opposition deliver blistering combos that can decimate your HP bar and give you far fewer windows to attack.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete Invincible VS Trophy List

Here’s a rough estimate of how long we think each difficulty should take you.

If you do complete the Invincible VS Story Mode, please let us know how long it took you to beat it and on which difficulty you managed to achieve this. The place to do so is our ever-active Insider Gaming Discord.

For confirmed upcoming content, check out Invincible VS story and character pass details being revealed.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: ARC Raiders Review, But Six Months Later
item.content:encoded:
When I reviewed ARC Raiders in October 2025, I dubbed it ‘the most refreshing extraction shooter ever made’ and awarded it perfect marks, 10 out of 10. It might come as no surprise to learn that ARC Raiders has ebbed, flowed, and evolved since then, and six months later, it’s time to look back at the game and see where it stands in the market.

Is ARC Raiders still ‘as good as it was’ when it first came out six months ago? Is it worth the investment of time, effort, and money it takes to understand the gameplay loop? Or have too many cacophonous changes and the foreboding lull of live-service shooters taken too hard a toll on Embark Studios’ smash-hit title?

Let’s find out. This is my ARC Raiders review, but six months later.

ARC Raiders Has Changed, But It’s Fundamentally The Same Game

ARC Raiders technically isn’t an extraction shooter, but an extraction adventure game. The term was coined by Embark Studios ahead of the game coming out, and it reflects on the game’s insistence on promoting cinematic sequences, collaboration, and social interaction over the cold-blooded brutality typically associated with the extraction space.

When it dropped in October 2025, ARC Raiders fully disrupted the market, pulling in dramatic numbers across all platforms, including social and streaming sites. It remained at the top of the charts for weeks, dominating conversations, securing the attention of the world’s biggest content creators, and fully remodelling what it meant to be an extraction game.

I fell in love with the game after eons of sitting firmly in the heart of the genre, and it became my top game of the year. I sang about ARC Raiders from the rooftops, pledging my adoration for the title to anyone who would lend me their ear.

But inevitably, things started to peter off.

While the core gameplay loop in ARC Raiders remains mostly the same today, the vibe around the title has shifted considerably, especially in recent weeks. You still load up, load in, do your quests, loot the map clean, fight, and make your way out of the raid; that’s never going to change, but the sentiment around this extraction adventure title certainly has.

The fundamentals are unaltered. Sure, there are more map conditions, new ARC enemies to fight, and a couple more maps since launch, but you play the game the same way you did on day one.

But what do I mean about the vibe and sentiment around ARC Raiders?

Embark Studios made plenty of changes very early on in ARC Raiders, building on community feedback to iterate quickly. The first new map arrived within weeks, backed by a game-wide initiative to unlock it, and things felt phenomenal. There was a sense of collaboration everywhere you looked; people were in their honeymoon phase, and everything was fresh and exciting.

Six months later, that’s not necessarily the case. We’ve only just gotten our second map, and it was delivered in an underwhelming fashion that failed to do much for the game’s dwindling sense of dominance. It was supposed to herald the grand ending of a four-month roadmap, but it felt like less of a bang and more of a fizzle for the content streak.

The game’s first Expedition loop was a neat take on the extraction genre, giving players control over wiping their account, but it has easily become one of the most contentious, divisive topics in the space, especially following the most recent, drastic changes from Embark Studios.

In the last few months, ARC Raiders has been plagued fairly hard by recurring problems, exploits, and bugs. While Embark Studios typically stays on top of patching these things out, it always seems like there’s another concern to consider when loading into a raid.

In the six months since ARC Raiders dropped, we’ve seen duplication exploits, out-of-map tricks, imbalances in the economy, crashes on certain platforms, crossplay functions failing, and a grind model that caters more to hardcore gamers than the casual market.

That’s another huge issue with ARC Raiders: the PvP vs. PvE debate.

It’s never-ending, and it has proven to be the most tiresome thing about this game. It’s a 50-50 split, and each side feels like they’re firmly in the right, when really, there’s no one right way to play ARC Raiders. It has hampered the community incessantly over the months, producing a tangible split right down the middle and harming the game’s overall impression.

Again, the core loop remains the same, but these issues present constant worries for players that too much polish is always needed to keep things running smoothly. This has resulted in what many feel is a cut-down roadmap and lacklustre delivery of new content.

Becoming Weaker with Time

Time is the one thing that ultimately brings all games crashing down. Nothing lasts forever; it’s just a question of how long you can keep your game at the top of the charts. For Embark Studios and ARC Raiders, the winding down of the success streak seems to be happening sooner than everyone might have wanted.

I’m not saying the game doesn’t have enough meat, but there’s not at all enough of an endgame circuit for the experienced player. Once you’ve completed the quests, all of which are drastically simple and run-of-the-mill, you have very little to accomplish.

The regular updates that offer users construction projects or special events are short-lived and easily beatable in a day, in some cases. Even wiping your account with an Expedition feels drab, as it just results in you needing to climb the ladder from scratch all over again. This has led to the inevitable collapse of the game’s player and viewer counts.

I will say that, if you’re a brand-new player and walking into ARC Raiders for the first time, you’ve probably got a good 50, 60 hours of content ready and waiting. That’s the advantage of getting into a live-service shooter six months after launch. It has evolved to the point where you have much more to do than the first adopters of the game.

That’s the saving grace of this retrospective ARC Raiders review. If you’ve already put 100 hours into it and you’re bored with it, that’s likely not going to change. If we’ve seen anything thus far, it’s that the updates aren’t sizeable or meaningful enough to really retain or (even better) grow the player count.

But if you’ve never played ARC Raiders and want an easy introduction to the extraction space, this is the one. It looks great on the surface, boasts one of the best soundtracks I’ve ever heard, and offers cinematic battles, a unique level of tension, and a diverse enough array of weapons, items, and enemies to keep you learning for a good few hours.

And, of course, it’s an extraction game after all, so the ‘gambling loop’ is front and centre. You’ll want to keep opening one more box, running one more raid, fighting one more enemy (player or ARC).

ARC Raiders Six-Month Review Verdict

Having done everything possible in ARC Raiders, I have to say that there’s almost no incentive for me to play the game anymore. Every time there’s a new update, I’m able to get on and soak up what has been introduced in a matter of hours, and then I cut away for another month until the next major patch.

It’s still a great game with solid bones, and Embark Studios is a pretty dedicated developer, but I just can’t see it dominating the space for years to come. The player counts are still looking great for the six-month mark, but it feels like the overall energy of the thing is dissipating, and folks are moving on in droves.

For new players, it’s well worth the price tag, but for anyone who has given it the time of day and is wondering if it’s worth coming back… Well, that’s another story entirely. I think I got my money’s worth, but I’ll never feel the same excitement I had when I experienced ARC Raiders in the game’s first week on the market.

Original Score: 10/10 (Masterpiece)

Six-Month Score: 8/10 (Great)

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server what you think about ARC Raiders now

For more Insider Gaming reviews, check out our coverage of Tides of Tomorrow

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: INDUSTRIA 2 Devs Apologize As Game Launches to Awful Reviews: ‘We Got Work To Do’
item.content:encoded:
Bleakmill has just released INDUSTRIA 2, the FPS follow-up to the 2021 title that introduced the mysterious, twisted universe. While the first game enjoyed decent reviews and a long tail, the sequel has been slammed with negativity right out of the gate.

The reviews on Steam paint a paltry picture for INDUSTRIA 2, which many have dubbed unfinished and broken. In a statement on social media, the developers at Bleakmill agreed that the feedback is fair, but they stressed that they’d put ‘all our hearts and souls’ into bringing INDUSTRIA 2 to life over the last four years.

INDUSTRIA 2 Falters From the Start

INDUSTRIA was a minuscule game when it dropped in 2021, emerging as an indie adventure that planted players firmly at the heart of a parallel Cold War-themed universe. It was a haunting shooter that secured fairly positive reviews, but it seems it’s not a case of INDUSTRIA walking so INDUSTRIA 2 could run.

On social media, the team at Bleakmill drew attention to the terrible reviews facing INDUSTRIA 2:

It hurts to be sitting at 48% positive ratings after putting all our hearts and souls into this game over 4 years. So much struggle, joy and difficulties our tiny team has overcome.

The critique is fair. Sorry to let you down everyone.

We got work to do. Only forward from here.

Bizarrely, the INDUSTRIA 2 demo has much more positive reviews than the full game, and it’s clear why from even the most cursory of glances at the game’s feedback.

The game-breaking bugs, placeholder assets, holes in the maps, audio blips, and many other problems have all weighed heavily on those investing money in the game, which has dropped with a price point of $22.49.

One of the most damning reviews came from a user who has accumulated 882 games on Steam, but only reviewed INDUSTRIA 2. They wrote:

I never leave reviews on games but Industria 2 is so disappointingly bad that I must warn others to steer clear of it. Saying this game feels rushed and unfinished is an understatement. If I could refund this game I would but fortunately for the devs I pressed past the 2 hour mark so my money is theirs now. Hopefully they can put that money to good use and fix the glaring issues with this game.

Fans of the first game have expressed their disappointment at waiting almost five years for this new product to surface, only to be overwhelmingly let down by what has surfaced.

Do you think you’d give INDUSTRIA 2 a try if it manages to recover? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insidre Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that experimental changes have landed in Escape from Tarkov

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: All Free Characters in Neverness to Everness & How to Get Them
item.content:encoded:
Neverness to Everness has a stacked roster of characters available to choose from, and while the vast majority come through pulls and RNG, there’s a host of free characters to claim.

The free-to-play gacha title has garnered plenty of interest, with over 35 million players worldwide pre-registering for Neverness to Everness, which could follow the likes of Genshin Impact and Wuthering Waves as the next gacha hit.

If you’re looking to ensure you get the best value for money by unlocking all the free characters, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Neverness to Everness Free Characters

There are currently six characters available for free in Neverness to Everness through the main progression path, which are set to be permanently available.

The majority are available through progression in the main story, including Zero, the player-controlled character, Mint, and Adler.

  • More Insider Gaming: Can You Romance Characters in Neverness to Everness?

Additional rewards come from Gacha pulls, including a guaranteed first pull, login rewards, and the City Tycoon system, while there’s the scope for more free characters to be added later down the line.

The table below outlines all the free characters in Neverness to Everness, and how to get them.

Can You Get More Free Characters in Neverness to Everness

Yes, there is an additional free character to earn in Neverness to Everness, with the exact outcome depending on player choice.

After using 50 pulls on the main board, each player is provided with a choice to earn one S-Rank character from the available quota, but this is an account reward, and not a gacha pity system.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Spawn Headless Rider Anomaly Boss in Neverness to Everness

The list of characters available in the selection consists of Baicang, Daffodil, Fadia, Hathor, Jiuyan, and Sakiri, all of whom have different strengths and weaknesses.

The choice is ultimately down to each individual player and the team composition you want to build, and there could be additional free characters made available in future.

What’s your favourite feature in Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Neverness to Everness, find out how to play with friends in multiplayer, and check out all of the active codes to redeem.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Saros Main Actor Makes Sly Dig at Review on Stream
item.content:encoded:
One of Saros’ main voice actors has made an apparent sly dig at one of the reviews of the game, referencing a 7/10 score given for the title.

Saros released on April 30, exclusively on PlayStation 5, was a hit with critics, securing a ‘generally favorable’ Metascore on Metacritic, with 96% positive reviews and just 4% mixed, though some did hover at the lower end.

IGN was among the lowest-scoring reviewers for the game, issuing one of the four 7/10 ratings, which stands as the lowest-rated, and an apparent dig has been made.

Rahul Kohli Makes Apparent Dig at IGN

On a Twitch livestream on Saros’ launch, Rahul Kohli, who portrays Arjen Devraj in Saros, streamed the game wearing an IGN 7/10 ‘good’ T-shirt, which was incidentally the exact rating the platform gave the title.

Kohli wore the t-shirt alongside Alanah Pearce, a former IGN employee, adding further layers to the apparent dig, and it didn’t take long for Reddit and social media to catch wind of the stream, with various posts and threads being made.

Though review scores are a metric that varies from outlet to outlet, and represents only one opinion, IGN has regularly earned clapback on social media for scores, including an infamous review in 2014 of Pokémon Alpha Sapphire and Omega Ruby, which declared the title had ‘too much water,’ spawning off into various memes.

It’s unclear whether Kohli’s attire was intentional or if he was even aware of the score and review that IGN gave, but it has certainly caused a chuckle in the community.

What do you think of Kohli’s attire in the Twitch stream? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more on Saros, read about how the game reviews make it a Game of the Year contender, and check out the full list of trophies.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: 7 New Invincible VS DLC Characters Have Leaked, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Invincible VS is the new fighting game kid on the block, but the series itself is anything but. The franchise has over two decades of history, which is why seven new Invincible VS characters have supposedly leaked already.

There’s been a lot of hype for Invincible VS. Its lineage doesn’t quite stretch to the outrageous lengths that Marvel or DC do, but there’s plenty for developer Quarter Up to fall back on.

Invincible VS Getting At Least 7 New DLC Characters & New Skins Revealed

Official Invincible VS story details and character pass details were revealed before the game’s full launch.

If we cross over into the ‘unofficial’ realm of gossip and leaks, MultiverSusie—who had a long-standing reputation for leaking Multiversus and now different games—has unveiled a list of upcoming Invincible VS characters.

The unannounced names allegedly include:

  • Agent Spider
  • Angstrom
  • Damien Darkblood
  • Dinosaurus
  • Mauler Twins
  • Oliver
  • Volcanikka

It is interesting that given the two characters we know have been announced (The Immortal and Universa) are supposed to be part of Year 1, this means anywhere between two to four years of DLC characters have now been leaked already!

If the leaks are true, Quarter Up could always end up pivoting, and we’ll update you if this is the case.

The leaker has also shown a look at unreleased character skins as well.

Again, MultiverSusie has been right about leaks in the past, but keep your expectations in check for the time being.

With Mortal Kombat, Tekken, and Street Fighter comfortably on the Mount Rushmore of fighters, does Invincible VS have the potential to join them? Let us know what you think of the game through our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more on the fighter, catch up on the game’s launch patch, which tweaks move sets and addresses crashes, and read through the complete Invincible VS Trophy List, if you haven’t already.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Full Fortnite x Star Wars May 2026 Roadmap: LEGO Fortnite, Mandalorian & Grogu, & More
item.content:encoded:
May is all about Star Wars in Fortnite, with the Epic Games collab continuing with the beloved franchise, and a full month of content is arriving, as outlined in an official roadmap.

Fortnite‘s close ties with Disney are well documented, and there is a long history of Star Wars collabs in the game, and while there may not be as many major headlines as previous years, which have seen major takeovers, there’s still plenty to dig your teeth into.

If you don’t want to miss a single piece of the content that Fortnite has in May for Star Wars, Insider Gaming has all the details.

Complete Fortnite x Star Wars May 2026 Roadmap

Fortnite has a wide array of Star Wars collab content arriving throughout May, with the majority being additions to the vast world of Creative.

Several major Star Wars game modes are releasing as Creative launches, including Galactic Siege, Escape Vader, and Droid Tycoon, alongside other user-created content.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Preload Forza Horizon 6—File Size, Explained

The other major game mode receiving collab content is LEGO Fortnite Odyssey, landing on May 14, while there are several releases planned in the Item Shop.

Check out the table below for all the Star Wars content arriving in Fortnite this month.

Is There a Fortnite x Star Wars Battle Royale Collab?

No, there is no official direct Fortnite collab with Star Wars in Battle Royale terms, unlike previous years, with the majority of content arriving outside of those modes.

Last year, a mini-season themed around Star Wars took over the Battle Royale scene, with some POIs remaining throughout the chapter, but that’s not the case this year.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Is The Next Tarkov Wipe?—Summer 2026 EFT Wipe

The limited scope of Battle Royale involvement merely extends to skins and cosmetics released in the item shop, including Clone Wars items and the Mandalorian Pen & Ink set, which can be used in Battle Royale modes.

However, don’t expect to see things like official Star Wars weapons, including Lightsabers, within the Battle Royale modes themselves.

What do you think about the Star Wars content coming to Fortnite? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at Microsoft outlining several ROG Xbox Ally Updates, and check out the free availability of Hogwarts Legacy.

MOBILE APP
